title: hypochaeris radicata subsp. salina
intro: Hypochaeris radicata subsp. salina, also known as sea dandelion, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antibacterial effects, which can be attributed to its high content of inulin and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of sea dandelion include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve kidney function, and aid in the management of digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Sea dandelion can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots can also be used in cooking and as a food supplement.
